stage.name: Commit: why architectural state must land in order
stage.description: A commit-stage lesson on architectural state, register and CSR updates, stores, precise exceptions, stalls, flushes, and the difference between result completion and retirement.
stage.courseTemplateName: Professional Technical Deep Dive - Evidence-Grounded SlideKit

scenes.title: Commit is where results become architectural truth

content.title: Commit: why architectural state must land in order
content.subtitle: Execution may produce results earlier, but commit decides what becomes visible architectural state.
content.thesis: Commit is the stage that keeps a complex backend honest to the programmer-visible machine.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: Commit is where the machine becomes accountable. Execute can produce a result, but that result is not automatically architectural truth. Commit decides when register state, CSR state, stores, exceptions, stalls, and flushes become visible in a precise way. If issue and execute are the busy workshop, commit is the inspector at the exit door.

actions.text: Commit is where the machine becomes accountable. Execute can produce a result, but that result is not automatically architectural truth. Commit decides when register state, CSR state, stores, exceptions, stalls, and flushes become visible in a precise way. If issue and execute are the busy workshop, commit is the inspector at the exit door.

scenes.title: Commit is the final stage of the spine

content.title: Final stage does not mean passive stage
content.subtitle: Commit receives completed work and controls what is allowed to update architectural state.

callouts.title: Input
callouts.body: Completed instruction information arrives from backend tracking.

callouts.title: State update
callouts.body: Register-file, CSR, and store commitment happen under commit control.

callouts.title: Control response
callouts.body: Exceptions, stalls, and flushes are handled precisely at this boundary.

content.takeaway: Commit is the bridge from microarchitecture to architectural contract.

assets.alt: Official CVA6 architecture overview block diagram

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: architecture and modules
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The architecture block diagram groups FRONTEND, DECODE, ISSUE, EXECUTE, COMMIT, CONTROLLER, and CSRFILE as the core reading map.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: On the map, commit sits at the end, but that does not make it passive. It is the bridge from microarchitecture to architectural contract. A result may exist internally, but commit decides whether and how it updates visible state. This is why commit is central to exception precision and to sane debug reasoning.

actions.text: On the map, commit sits at the end, but that does not make it passive. It is the bridge from microarchitecture to architectural contract. A result may exist internally, but commit decides whether and how it updates visible state. This is why commit is central to exception precision and to sane debug reasoning.

content.title: Retire the oldest valid work first
content.subtitle: In-order retirement lets the core expose a precise architectural story.

steps.title: Select committable entry
steps.body: The oldest completed instruction is considered for retirement.

steps.title: Write visible state
steps.body: Register-file and CSR updates are allowed at the proper architectural point.

steps.title: Commit stores
steps.body: Memory side effects become official only when the instruction retires.

steps.title: Handle exceptions
steps.body: Exception causes are processed precisely against the associated instruction.

steps.title: Control stall or flush
steps.body: Commit can drive pipeline control when architectural ordering requires it.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: Commit controls several things at once. It selects work that is ready to retire, allows register and CSR updates, commits stores, handles exceptions, and can drive stalls or flushes. The common theme is ordering. The machine may have done work internally, but the outside story must still look like instructions retired in the correct program order.

actions.text: Commit controls several things at once. It selects work that is ready to retire, allows register and CSR updates, commits stores, handles exceptions, and can drive stalls or flushes. The common theme is ordering. The machine may have done work internally, but the outside story must still look like instructions retired in the correct program order.

scenes.title: Precise exceptions need a retirement boundary

content.title: Detecting an exception is not the same as taking it
content.subtitle: Earlier stages can attach exception evidence; commit makes the architectural decision precise.

columns.title: Before commit
columns.body: Fetch, decode, issue, and execute can discover or carry exception-related evidence with an instruction.

columns.title: At commit
columns.body: The exception is considered against the instruction at retirement, so visible state remains precise.

content.takeaway: The retirement boundary is what keeps the exception story debuggable.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: Precise exceptions are one of those phrases people repeat without unpacking. The useful version is this: earlier stages may discover exception evidence, but commit considers the exception when the related instruction reaches retirement. That way, older instructions have retired, younger instructions have not polluted architectural state, and software gets a coherent trap story.

actions.text: Precise exceptions are one of those phrases people repeat without unpacking. The useful version is this: earlier stages may discover exception evidence, but commit considers the exception when the related instruction reaches retirement. That way, older instructions have retired, younger instructions have not polluted architectural state, and software gets a coherent trap story.

content.title: Commit guards more than the integer register file
content.subtitle: A complete explanation includes CSR state, stores, and control effects.

cards.title: Register file
cards.body: Integer-visible results become part of architectural state at retirement.

cards.title: CSR file
cards.body: CSR writes and related side effects are committed in the controlled stage.

cards.title: Stores
cards.body: Memory side effects are committed only when the instruction is allowed to retire.

cards.title: Pipeline control
cards.body: Flushes and stalls protect the architectural contract when the path changes.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: Commit guards more than one door. The register file is the obvious door. CSR state is another. Stores are critical because memory side effects cannot be casually undone. Pipeline control is also part of the story, because flushes and stalls protect the contract when an exception or redirect changes what younger work means.

actions.text: Commit guards more than one door. The register file is the obvious door. CSR state is another. Stores are critical because memory side effects cannot be casually undone. Pipeline control is also part of the story, because flushes and stalls protect the contract when an exception or redirect changes what younger work means.

content.title: Ask what becomes visible
content.subtitle: Commit is about visibility, order, and precision.

items.title: What is the oldest ready entry?
Which state is updated?
items.body: Register, CSR, memory store, and control state must be named separately.

items.title: Is there an exception?
items.body: Tie exception handling to the instruction that is retiring.

items.title: Is a flush or stall required?
items.body: Commit can control the pipeline to preserve architectural correctness.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: When reading commit, ask what becomes visible. Which entry is oldest and ready? Which state is updated? Is an exception attached to that retiring instruction? Does the pipeline need to stall or flush? These questions turn commit from a vague final box into the stage that enforces the contract.

actions.text: When reading commit, ask what becomes visible. Which entry is oldest and ready? Which state is updated? Is an exception attached to that retiring instruction? Does the pipeline need to stall or flush? These questions turn commit from a vague final box into the stage that enforces the contract.

questions.question: What is commit primarily responsible for?

options.label: Retiring completed work and updating architectural state in a controlled order
Predicting the next PC before fetch
Choosing the Git branch for deployment

questions.analysis: Commit controls retirement and visible architectural updates.

questions.question: Which kinds of state or side effects can be part of commit reasoning?
questions.analysis: Commit is responsible for multiple visible-state and control concerns.

questions.question: Why distinguish result completion from commit?

options.label: A result can exist internally before it is safe to expose it as architectural state

questions.analysis: Internal result availability is not the same as retirement.

questions.question: What does a precise exception require?

options.label: The exception is associated with the retiring instruction so older and younger state remain coherent
All younger instructions must retire first

questions.analysis: Precise exceptions depend on retirement ordering and controlled state update.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: The commit quiz is about visibility. Results, side effects, and exceptions become public facts only when the architectural contract allows it. That is why commit matters even in an introductory architecture course. Without it, the backend story is fast but not trustworthy.

actions.text: The commit quiz is about visibility. Results, side effects, and exceptions become public facts only when the architectural contract allows it. That is why commit matters even in an introductory architecture course. Without it, the backend story is fast but not trustworthy.

content.title: You can now separate completion from retirement
content.subtitle: This is the core idea behind precise architectural behavior.
takeaways: Commit updates architectural state in order.
Register, CSR, store, exception, stall, and flush behavior all belong in the commit discussion.
Earlier-stage exception evidence becomes precise when handled at the retirement boundary.
content.nextStep: Next: Checkpoint 03, where you prove the whole pipeline spine is ordered and source-grounded.
content.closing: Commit is where a busy microarchitecture keeps its promise to software.

sourceAnchors.title: CV32A60X design: COMMIT_STAGE module
sourceAnchors.claimSupported: The commit stage updates architectural state, commits stores, writes CSR and register-file state, manages stalling/flushing, and handles precise exceptions at commit.

content.speakerNotes: Commit closes the spine. You can now separate a completed result from a retired architectural update. That distinction is what makes the backend understandable. Next comes the Unit 03 checkpoint. It will ask you to order stages, place responsibilities, and avoid the classic mistakes around issue, execute, and commit.
